physicalDetails.detail: Narrow-faced high elf, pale as ashwood, introduced already mounted with her bow across her knees and readiness visible before she speaks.
Chapter 3 keeps her visually spare but places her at the edges of water and road: crouched by the stream, then farther up the north road where the ditch preserves clearer prints.
Chapter 4 keeps her as a quiet edge-of-frame presence: positioned at lane mouths, doors, and suspect grain lines, doing the watchful work that lets everyone else turn inward.

Chapter 5 keeps her all edge and threshold: peeling right on command, crossing warehouse doors a heartbeat after danger is named, and turning route choice into horseflesh and packing before anyone can waste words on it.

Chapter 6 gives Lyriel more visible personality without changing her edge-work: she rides the right flank with an eager forward lean, mouth set at camp, then lifts her chin a fraction the moment Jaina finally chooses the road again.

Chapter 7 keeps Lyriel quick and visible when visibility helps: right-side coverage in the queue, then grain-dusted from the yards and leaning toward the next movement before the capture plan is even finished.

Chapter 8 keeps Lyriel quick at the edge of motion: hand on the cart horse before it can shy, body flowing to lane mouths and roof stairs, then back into the shelter with the same forward tilt she always carries into the next problem.

Chapter 9 finally puts Lyriel in crisp close-quarter field light: right flank on the road, plaster across one side of her face in the farmhouse fight, and then bare absence where she stood once the corridor ward fires.
Chapter 11 keeps Lyriel on the page only as absence: the missing shape on the right flank that Cyndia checks for twice and Sylvanas finally looks at without looking away.

voiceMarkers.detail: No direct dialogue on page in chapter 2; competence is her first voice marker, expressed through speed of draw and clean response to command.
When she speaks in chapter 3 it is in short, useful negatives and spoor notes — no bodies in the stream, clearer prints here, they didn't walk like living people.
Chapter 4 again makes silence part of her profile; she contributes through placement, transfer, and watchfulness more than speech.

Her Chapter 5 dialogue is sparse, observational, and already filtered for usefulness: fresh cut at the gate, no stable hands, east fork too quiet, one abandoned cart, the kind of report that changes decisions without ornament.

Her Chapter 6 speech adds dry commentary to her usual brevity — preferring to leave now if they are leaving, joking that she would have preferred a cleaner destination, and admitting the corruption through the horse before anyone wants to call it aloud.
Her Chapter 7 dialogue stays dry and economical, but now it carries city-watch specificity: hand signals, not-local street talk, and the practical question of where a second body should fall.
She remains verbally spare in chapter 8; what sharpens instead is how quickly her warnings and small practical acts slot into the larger movement of the team.
Chapter 9 gives her some of her clearest on-page lines — dry absence-checks, blunt convenience-calls, and quick enemy counts — before the trap cuts the voice off entirely.

beliefs.belief: Escort discipline and fast shots keep the formation alive.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Supported by how she behaves on the road and in the wildlife attack.

beliefs.belief: Absence is evidence; water not taking bodies matters as much as finding bodies would.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed by her stream sweep in chapter 3.

beliefs.belief: Holding the edge of a scene matters because someone always has to watch the way out while others look inward.
A road gone too quiet is as informative as a crowded one, especially once the enemy begins cleaning ahead of pursuit.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed in chapter 5 by her east-fork report and depot perimeter findings.

beliefs.belief: Once the useful answer is clear, lingering in another commander's camp is worse than riding into ugly truth on your own terms.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Supported by her immediate preference for departure after Arthas's refusal.

beliefs.belief: In a live-city grab, contingency is part of discipline; the second body matters before the first one even drops.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed by her chapter 7 planning question and perimeter role.

beliefs.belief: In a city operation the next body, next horse, and next corner matter before the current problem is fully finished.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed in chapter 8 through her handling of the horse, the window line, and the roof stair during the escape.

beliefs.belief: The edge has to be called early and dry, because the team is safest when someone notices what is missing before anyone explains it.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed throughout the chapter up to the instant the trap kills her.

goalsHistory.active: Stay with the escort and cover the approach into Millhaven.
goalsHistory.underlying: Keep the formation intact under Sylvanas's command.

goalsHistory.active: Remain on Sylvanas's southern track and keep reading the road's edges for clearer signs.
goalsHistory.underlying: Help the reduced party survive and move faster than the dead.

goalsHistory.active: Stay on the moving edge of the pursuit toward Andorhal, holding doors, prisoners, and contaminated stock lines where needed.
goalsHistory.underlying: Keep the quartet's momentum from being broken by local chaos.

goalsHistory.active: Hold the edge security of the southbound pursuit and turn small route details into usable warning before the formation blunders into them.
goalsHistory.underlying: Keep the quartet's momentum intact by making sure someone is always reading the side lanes, gates, and exits.

goalsHistory.active: Hold the right-side edge and keep the approach readable as the quartet enters Andorhal quietly.
goalsHistory.underlying: Make sure somebody is still noticing the horseflesh, hedges, skyline, and gate flow while Jaina and Sylvanas read bigger patterns.

goalsHistory.active: Hold the tighter right-side line of the Andorhal operation and be ready for the second problem the moment the first target moves.
goalsHistory.underlying: Keep the capture from turning into a city alarm because someone forgot the next body, next lane, or next signal.

goalsHistory.active: Stay with the core field unit as it leaves Andorhal for the next sweep eastward.
goalsHistory.underlying: Keep reading the edge conditions that let the faster minds and heavier hands do their work without getting surprised at the side.

goalsAtChapterEnd.active: None — Lyriel is killed instantly by the compound ward before the mission can continue.
goalsAtChapterEnd.underlying: She dies still doing right-flank work for the team.

emotionalStateHistory.state: Controlled combat focus; the prose gives no interiority beyond readiness and professional steadiness.
Controlled and alert, with the same professional steadiness now turned toward spoor and moving danger rather than surprise attack.
Still controlled and alert; chapter 4 gives her no visible emotional wobble, only continued readiness.
Quietly alert and unchanged in discipline; chapter 5 gives her more evidence work, not more visible strain.
A little more openly strained than before, but only in wit and preference for motion; discipline still holds the line.
Alert, dry-humored, and fully keyed to immediate action; chapter 7 lets her strain show as readiness rather than hesitation.
Fully keyed to motion, still dry and alert, with chapter 8 proving she can carry urban contingency at the same speed she once carried roadside warning.

emotionalStateAtChapterEnd.state: Not applicable. Lyriel dies in the trap corridor before any end-of-chapter emotional state can replace action.

decisionsThisChapter.detail: Took left-verge coverage during Jaina's roadside reading and loosed first on the charging deer.
Went after the wounded wolf without ceremony once it dragged itself into the grass.
Checked the stream for bodies and later pushed farther up the north road to find clearer preserved prints near the ditch.
Helped move Tomas under armed control, held the shed door during the attempted interrogation, and later oversaw the suspect sacks while the captain's men separated them.
Took the right-side perimeter at the stripped depot, then brought in the rear tally board that still held enough scoring to recover route information.
Reported the east fork as unnaturally quiet and helped lock the final decision onto the south road.
Handled the immediate practical consequences of the route choice by tightening Jaina's second pack and turning departure into action before dawn.
Pressed for immediate departure once it was clear the camp would not give them what they needed, turning impatience into useful momentum.
Stayed on the right and kept reading the approach as the corruption thickened, then marked the skyline response the moment Andorhal finally came into view.
Reports the north-yard pair trading not-local marks, then takes the tighter right-side security lane for the capture plan.
Catches the cart horse before the capture can turn into noise and visibility.
Holds the back-window line and then the forward lane line during the escape, helping keep the group from choosing the obvious bad exit.
Keeps civilians from becoming additional complications, most clearly when she rights the baker's apprentice instead of letting panic propagate.
Takes rear coverage on the farmhouse stop, then the right side of the breakout and north-wall escape.
Keeps reporting enemy placement and physical escape facts instead of dramatizing the pressure.
Moves with the formation into the compound dip and dies when the first ward is keyed to disciplined scout spacing.

abilitiesDemonstrated.ability: Fast target acquisition and immediate release under surprise pressure.
Maintains flank coverage while the rest of the party is split between command, horses, and magical diagnosis.
Reads water and roadside preservation conditions well enough to eliminate false assumptions and find a cleaner spoor line.
Holds security on confined scenes without stealing focus — prisoner transfer, door watch, suspect-stock oversight.
Reads fresh wheel cuts and yard disturbance well enough to tell that one loaded team left the depot southbound in a hurry.
Salvages useful evidence from damaged infrastructure, bringing in the scraped tally board that helps recover the feeder-road line.
Translates command decisions into immediate travel readiness, especially around horses, packs, and departure sequence.
Registers strain in mounts and road atmosphere quickly enough to voice the approach pressure before the city is fully visible, helping confirm Jaina's magical read with ordinary field observation.
Reads covert signaling inside ordinary labor traffic, distinguishing rehearsed operative marks from local yard habit.
Stabilizes the moving environment around a snatch, including horse, lane mouth, and window line, before panic can broadcast what happened.
Transitions cleanly between street line and roof line during pursuit, giving the unit flexible edge coverage inside dense city terrain.
Handles startled civilians with the same economy she brings to tactical motion, reducing noise without wasting time on reassurance theater.
Reads absence fast enough to flag the wrongness of a road by what is not there as much as by what is.
Supplies quick enemy-count and exit-route information under close ambush conditions.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Cyndia
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: Names her matter-of-factly while distributing horses and formation positions.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Sylvanas
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: Uses her name as a clean field assignment marker during the village sweep.

bodyLanguagePatterns.detail: Arrow already nocked, left-side coverage active, and almost no wasted movement between threat recognition and release.
Crouches low over terrain details instead of dramatizing them, then rises and reports only what the ground or water truly gives.
Chapter 4 keeps her in threshold posture — looking away when Jaina and Sylvanas snap at each other, taking the door, keeping the suspect sacks under her eye while everyone else speaks.

Chapter 5 keeps her in threshold posture even more strongly than before: right wall, warehouse door, rear rail, then already moving on the horses while other people are still finishing the discussion.

Chapter 6 keeps her in taut threshold posture but lets more attitude show through it: forward lean on the right flank, mouth set in camp, chin lifting once the order to move lands, and a low involuntary sound at first sight of the working city.

Chapter 7 keeps her in forward-tilted threshold posture: appearing behind Cyndia with the next observation ready, then leaning into the practical problem before other people can start theorizing about it.
Moves ahead of the unit in short disappear-and-return bursts, making the lane feel readable by the time the others reach it.
Keeps the same forward lean even in the shelter, as if still listening for the next turn in the route.
Carries the same forward-tilted edge-readiness from earlier chapters right up to the moment the corridor turns lethal.

knowledge.detail: Knows the southbound ride is not routine border travel and behaves accordingly from the moment the escort forms.
Knows there are no bodies in Millhaven's stream, no washdown, and that the north-road ditch preserves the dead's wrong gait more clearly than the churn behind the houses.
Learns at close range that the contaminated grain has to be watched physically even after discovery because villagers, guards, and suspects can all become part of the danger chain.
Knows the stripped depot still sent one loaded team south before the party arrived.
Knows the east fork has gone quieter than it should have, helping confirm the south feeder as the live line.
Knows the horses are feeling Andorhal's approach pressure before the city comes into full view.
Sees the first full skyline of a still-working Andorhal and understands immediately that the city is alive in the exact way that makes the situation worse.
Knows at least two workers in the north yard are exchanging disciplined marks that do not read as local casual talk, strengthening the case for an embedded cell.
Knows the city had active roof and street response bands in place within seconds of the operative's death.
Sees that Jaina and Sylvanas now function as one tempo under that pressure, which lets the perimeter pair keep their jobs distinct instead of collapsing into generic backup.
Leaves chapter 8 alive, intact, and still part of the four-woman traveling unit moving toward Eastweald.
Recognizes the farmhouse as too convenient, the road as birdless, and the north wall as the best immediate escape line once the ambush closes.
Leaves no later knowledge because the trap kills her instantly.
By chapter 11 her death remains active formation knowledge: the right side of the road is still being checked for a ranger who is no longer there.

arcHistory.currentPosition: Introduced as the quickest-shot flank ranger in Sylvanas's mounted detail.
Chapter 3 turns her from silent flank cover into the ranger who can tell Sylvanas where absence ends and a usable trail begins.
Chapter 4 keeps expanding her as the ranger who quietly holds the perimeter conditions that make other people's discoveries possible.
Chapter 5 deepens Lyriel's role as the quartet's edge reader: the ranger who notices the useful absence, recovers the overlooked board, and makes movement happen quickly once the choice is made.

Chapter 6 broadens Lyriel from edge reader into edge reader with a sharper voice: still disciplined, still useful first, but now willing to let a dry line or visible preference for motion show through the professionalism.
Chapter 7 broadens Lyriel from edge reader into contingency reader: the ranger who watches not just the lane but the second consequence waiting behind the first move.
Chapter 8 turns Lyriel from contingency-minded perimeter ranger into an urban edge reader who can keep horses, civilians, corners, and roofs from becoming the failure point.
Chapter 11 confirms that Lyriel’s posthumous role in continuity is behavioral rather than memorialized. She persists as changed drill on the road.

arc.currentPosition: Chapter 11 keeps Lyriel’s ending from fading into summary. The story still carries her as missing weight in the formation, proving the trap changed how the living move.
